title: HYPERICIN
description: Hypericin is a naphthodianthrone compound found in St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum), primarily used for treating mild to moderate depression. It acts as a weak MAO-A and MAO-B inhibitor, has affinity for sigma receptors, modulates HPA axis function, and antagonizes several receptors including GABA-A/B and benzodiazepine receptors. Often combined with hyperforin, another active constituent that inhibits reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine.

research.title: Effect of Hypericum perforatum (St John's Wort) in Major Depressive Disorder
research.summary: Clinical trial by the Hypericum Depression Trial Study Group examining the efficacy of St John's Wort extract for treating major depression, comparing it to placebo and standard antidepressants.

research.title: St John's wort (Hypericum perforatum L.): a review of its chemistry, pharmacology and clinical properties
research.summary: Comprehensive review of hypericin and hyperforin chemistry, mechanisms of action including MAO inhibition and receptor interactions, and clinical applications for depression treatment.

research.title: St. John's wort (Hypericum perforatum): a review of the current pharmacological, toxicological, and clinical literature
research.summary: Extensive review covering pharmacological mechanisms, potential toxicity concerns including photosensitivity, and clinical efficacy data for depression and anxiety disorders.

sentiment.summary: Users report moderate effectiveness for mild to moderate depression and anxiety, with benefits appearing after 2-4 weeks of consistent use. However, concerns about eye damage from hypericin, drug interactions, and inconsistent results between brands create hesitation, leading to genuinely mixed experiences.

effects.label: Delayed Onset:
effects.description: Benefits typically require 2-4 weeks of consistent daily use before noticeable effects appear, similar to prescription antidepressants

effects.label: Extract Variability:
effects.description: Effectiveness varies significantly between brands and extract types, with hypericin-standardized (0.3%) and hyperforin-standardized (2%) formulations showing different results

effects.label: Mild to Moderate Cases:
effects.description: Most effective for mild to moderate depression and anxiety; users with severe depression report limited benefits

effects.label: Individual Response:
effects.description: Highly variable individual responses, with some users experiencing significant benefits while others report minimal or no effects

dosage.label: Standard Daily Dose:
dosage.description: 900mg per day divided into three 300mg doses, standardized to 0.3% hypericin, is the most commonly referenced effective dosage

dosage.label: Higher Doses:
dosage.description: Some users report better results with 1800mg daily (in divided doses), though this increases side effect risk

dosage.label: Hyperforin Formulations:
dosage.description: Perika and similar brands standardized to 2% hyperforin (6mg) are mentioned as potentially safer alternatives regarding eye concerns

dosage.label: Consistency Required:
dosage.description: Daily consistent dosing for at least 2-4 weeks is necessary before assessing effectiveness

sideEffects.label: Photosensitivity and Eye Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Major worry about hypericin causing retinal damage or cataracts with light exposure, though risk with hyperforin formulations appears lower

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Significant interactions with medications including SSRIs, birth control, and various prescription drugs due to effects on liver enzymes

sideEffects.label: Digestive Issues:
sideEffects.description: Some users report appetite changes, nausea, or gastrointestinal discomfort, particularly when starting

sideEffects.label: Cognitive Effects:
sideEffects.description: Reports of brain fog, restlessness, impatience, or difficulty focusing in some users, especially in early weeks

availability.label: Widely Available OTC:
availability.description: Readily available over-the-counter at health stores and online retailers without prescription

availability.label: Brand Quality Varies:
availability.description: quality brands, quality brands (Perika), and standardized pharmaceutical-grade extracts are most commonly recommended for consistent potency

availability.label: Standardization Important:
availability.description: Users emphasize choosing products standardized to either 0.3% hypericin or 2% hyperforin for reliable effects
